claim: Self-training agents improve performance by collecting successful trajectories and using them to fine-tune or build training data
actionable: Capture successful agent runs (prompts, tool calls, outcomes) into a log, then use those trajectories to create a training dataset
source_title: Self-Training Agents: Hermes Agent, HF Traces, Skills, MCP & Finetuning  — Merve Noyan, Hugging Face

claim: Hugging Face Traces and Hero/Hermes agent frameworks provide abstractions for agent training loops
actionable: Use HF Traces to log agent trajectories, then feed into your fine-tuning pipeline or agent harness
source_title: Self-Training Agents: Hermes Agent, HF Traces, Skills, MCP & Finetuning  — Merve Noyan, Hugging Face

claim: Agent skills can be bundled with training data to create reusable, fine-tuned behavior for specific domains
actionable: When building a domain-specific agent skill, include both the SKILL.md definition and example trajectories for supervised fine-tuning
source_title: Self-Training Agents: Hermes Agent, HF Traces, Skills, MCP & Finetuning  — Merve Noyan, Hugging Face
